programming-languages.name: Programming Languages
programming-languages.description: Test your knowledge of programming language concepts, paradigms, and fundamentals.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a programming paradigm?
What does 'IDE' stand for?
Which programming language is considered the 'mother of all languages'?
What is a compiler?
options: A program that executes code line by line
A program that converts source code to machine code

questions.question: Which type of language is closest to machine code?
What is polymorphism in OOP?
options: Wrapping data in a single unit

questions.question: Which language is primarily used for iOS app development?
What is a variable in programming?
options: A named storage location for data

questions.question: Which of the following is a dynamically typed language?
What is recursion?
What does API stand for?
Which data structure follows LIFO principle?
What is encapsulation in OOP?
options: Wrapping data and methods in a single unit

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a markup language?
What is garbage collection?
What is a syntax error?
options: A violation of language grammar rules

questions.question: Which concept allows a class to inherit from multiple parent classes?
What is the purpose of a debugger?
options: To find and fix errors in code

questions.question: Which of these is a version control system?
What is an algorithm?
options: A step-by-step procedure to solve a problem

python.name: Python
python.description: Challenge yourself with Python programming questions covering basics to advanced concepts.

questions.question: What is the output of print(type(5))?
Which keyword is used to define a function in Python?
What does 'len()' function do in Python?
options: Calculates length of a string only
Returns the number of items in an object

questions.question: Which of the following is immutable in Python?
What is the correct way to create a list in Python?
What is PEP 8?
options: Python Enhancement Proposal for coding style

questions.question: What does the 'self' keyword refer to in Python classes?
options: The current instance of the class

questions.question: Which method is used to add an element to a list?
What is a Python decorator?
options: A function that modifies another function

questions.question: What is the output of print(2**3)?
Which library is used for data manipulation in Python?
What does 'pip' stand for?
What is a lambda function in Python?
Which keyword is used for exception handling in Python?
What is a Python dictionary?
What is the output of bool('')?
What is list comprehension?
options: A concise way to create lists

questions.question: Which of the following is used to read a file in Python?
What is a generator in Python?
options: A function that yields values lazily

questions.question: What does the 'pass' statement do?
options: Does nothing, acts as a placeholder

kotlin.name: Kotlin
kotlin.description: Evaluate your Kotlin programming skills with these comprehensive questions.

questions.question: Who developed Kotlin?
Which keyword is used to declare a variable that can be reassigned in Kotlin?
What is Kotlin's null safety feature?
options: Variables cannot be null by default
There is no null in Kotlin

questions.question: Which platform officially supports Kotlin for Android development?
What is a data class in Kotlin?
options: A class designed to hold data

questions.question: What does the '?.' operator do in Kotlin?
options: Safe call - executes only if not null

questions.question: Which function is the entry point of a Kotlin program?
What is a companion object in Kotlin?
options: An object declared inside a class for static-like behavior

questions.question: Which keyword is used for string templates in Kotlin?
What is a sealed class in Kotlin?
options: A class with restricted inheritance hierarchy

questions.question: What does the 'when' expression replace in Kotlin?
What is coroutine in Kotlin?
options: A lightweight thread for async programming

questions.question: Which keyword makes a class inheritable in Kotlin?
What is the Elvis operator in Kotlin?
What is an extension function in Kotlin?
options: A function that adds behavior to existing classes

questions.question: Which collection type is immutable by default in Kotlin?
What is the keyword to create a singleton in Kotlin?
What does the 'lazy' keyword do in Kotlin?
options: Initializes a property on first access

questions.question: Which operator is used for non-null assertion in Kotlin?
What is the difference between 'val' and 'var' in Kotlin?
options: val is for strings, var is for numbers
val is immutable, var is mutable
val is global, var is local

java.name: Java
java.description: Test your Java programming expertise from core concepts to advanced topics.

questions.question: What is the JVM?
Which keyword is used to create an object in Java?
What is the default value of an int variable in Java?
Which access modifier makes a member accessible only within its own class?
What is the super keyword used for in Java?
Which collection interface does NOT allow duplicate elements?
What is method overloading?
options: Multiple methods with same name but different parameters

questions.question: Which Java keyword is used to define a constant?
What is an abstract class in Java?
options: A class that cannot have methods
A class that cannot be instantiated and may have abstract methods
A class with only static methods

questions.question: Which exception type must be caught or declared?
What is the purpose of the 'this' keyword in Java?
Which of the following is a functional interface in Java?
What is the output of 'Hello'.charAt(1)?
What is a Java Stream?
options: A sequence of elements supporting operations

questions.question: Which keyword prevents method overriding?
What is autoboxing in Java?
options: Automatic conversion between primitives and wrapper classes

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'interface' keyword?
options: To define a contract that classes must implement

questions.question: Which class is the root of the Java class hierarchy?
What is a generic in Java?
What is the difference between == and .equals() in Java?

csharp.name: C#
csharp.description: Put your C# programming knowledge to the test with these challenging questions.

questions.question: Who created C#?
What is the CLR in C#?
Which keyword is used for inheritance in C#?
What is a namespace in C#?
Which data type is used for decimal precision in C#?
What is LINQ in C#?
What is a delegate in C#?
Which keyword is used for asynchronous methods in C#?
What is the purpose of the 'using' statement in C#?
options: Importing namespaces and managing resource disposal

questions.question: What is a property in C#?
options: A member that provides a flexible mechanism to read/write data

questions.question: What is the difference between 'ref' and 'out' parameters?
options: ref must be initialized before passing, out doesn't need to be
out must be initialized, ref doesn't
Both are the same as pass by value

questions.question: What is an event in C#?
options: A mechanism based on delegates for notifications

questions.question: Which .NET framework is cross-platform?
What is boxing in C#?
options: Converting reference type to value type
Converting value type to reference type

questions.question: What is a struct in C#?
options: A value type that can contain data members and methods

questions.question: Which keyword is used to prevent a class from being inherited?
What is dependency injection?
options: A design pattern for achieving IoC

questions.question: What is the null-conditional operator in C#?
What is an indexer in C#?
options: A member that allows object to be indexed like an array

questions.question: What is the 'var' keyword used for in C#?

cpp.name: C++
cpp.description: Test your C++ programming mastery from pointers to templates and beyond.

questions.question: Who created C++?
What is a pointer in C++?
options: A variable that stores memory address

questions.question: Which operator is used for dynamic memory allocation in C++?
What is the purpose of 'cout' in C++?
What is a reference in C++?
options: An alias for an existing variable

questions.question: Which header file is needed for input/output in C++?
What is a virtual function in C++?
options: A function that can be overridden in derived class

questions.question: What is the difference between struct and class in C++?
options: Default access: struct is public, class is private

questions.question: What is a destructor in C++?
options: A function called when an object is destroyed

questions.question: What is operator overloading?
What is a template in C++?
What is the Standard Template Library (STL)?
options: A collection of template classes and functions

questions.question: What does the 'const' keyword do?
options: Declares a constant value that cannot be modified

questions.question: What is a friend function in C++?
options: A function that can access private members of a class

questions.question: What is RAII in C++?
Which of these is NOT a type of inheritance in C++?
What is a smart pointer?
options: An object that manages dynamic memory automatically

questions.question: What is the scope resolution operator in C++?
What is a namespace used for in C++?
options: Organizing code and avoiding name conflicts

questions.question: What is move semantics in C++?
options: Transferring resources from one object to another efficiently
